headline: Opioid Substitution Therapy Waitlist Grows to 8,500 Patients
context: Methadone and buprenorphine treatment programs cannot accommodate growing demand from opioid-dependent patients.

cards.text: 8,500 patients on waitlist for opioid substitution therapy as of December 2025
cards.sub: Only 52,000 of estimated 170,000 opioid users receive any form of treatment

cards.h: Average wait time for methadone program entry is 4.5 months in Klang Valley
cards.s: Patients waiting for treatment face overdose risk 6 times higher than those in treatment
cards.d: Average wait time for methadone program entry is 4.5 months in Klang Valley Patients waiting for treatment face overdose risk 6 times higher than those in treatment

cards.h: Government methadone clinics operate only during office hours excluding working patients
cards.s: Rigid scheduling forces patients to choose between treatment and employment
cards.d: Government methadone clinics operate only during office hours excluding working patients Rigid scheduling forces patients to choose between treatment and employment

cards.h: France expanded OST through community pharmacies reaching 180,000 patients since 2018
cards.s: Pharmacy-based distribution dramatically increased treatment accessibility and retention
cards.d: France expanded OST through community pharmacies reaching 180,000 patients since 2018 Pharmacy-based distribution dramatically increased treatment accessibility and retention

cards.text: Making people wait months for addiction treatment is sentencing them to continued suffering
Like making diabetics queue 8 months for insulin: the treatment exists, the patients are identified, but the bottleneck is between them
cards.sub: Addiction is a medical condition with effective treatment; the waitlist converts a treatable condition into preventable suffering

cards.text: Should opioid substitution therapy be available through community pharmacies not just clinics
